default_action: Provide home care guidance and routine follow-up.

rules.reason: General danger signs in children require immediate life-saving treatment and emergency referral.
rules.recommended_action: Give pre-referral treatment (keep warm, give glucose if at risk, give diazepam if convulsing, start oxygen if distressed) and refer urgently to hospital.

rules.reason: Severe pneumonia with chest indrawing or stridor indicates very severe respiratory infection requiring immediate hospital care.
rules.recommended_action: Give first dose of IV/IM antibiotic and oxygen immediately, then refer urgently to hospital with pre-referral treatment.

symptomsAny: skin pinch goes back very slowly
rules.reason: Severe dehydration with danger signs requires IV rehydration and urgent hospital referral.
rules.recommended_action: Start IV rehydration with isotonic solution immediately and refer urgently to hospital with mother.

rules.reason: Fever with stiff neck suggests meningitis or severe bacterial infection requiring emergency treatment.
rules.recommended_action: Give first dose of IV/IM antibiotics immediately and refer urgently to hospital.

rules.reason: Severe anaemia indicated by severe palmar pallor is a danger sign requiring urgent assessment and possible transfusion.
rules.recommended_action: Refer urgently to hospital for assessment of other severe classifications and possible transfusion.

rules.reason: Complicated severe acute malnutrition with oedema indicates medical complications requiring inpatient hospital management.
rules.recommended_action: Refer urgently to hospital for inpatient therapeutic feeding and treatment of complications.

rules.reason: Moderate pneumonia without chest indrawing shows pneumonia signs but can be treated at clinic with close follow-up.
rules.recommended_action: Give oral amoxicillin for 5 days and teach mother to return urgently if breathing worsens.

rules.reason: Some dehydration with 2+ signs indicates moderate fluid loss treatable with oral rehydration and close monitoring.
rules.recommended_action: Give oral rehydration salts and zinc for 14 days, then return in 5 days for reassessment.

rules.reason: Confirmed malaria requires prompt first-line antimalarial treatment to prevent severe disease.
rules.recommended_action: Give artemether-lumefantrine or artesunate + amodiaquine, then return in 3 days if fever persists.

rules.reason: Fever without malaria detected requires identification of cause and appropriate treatment.
rules.recommended_action: Assess for other causes and give paracetamol for fever > 38.5°C, return if fever persists in 3 days.

rules.reason: Dysentery indicates bacterial infection requiring antibiotic treatment and fluid replacement.
rules.recommended_action: Give ciprofloxacin for 3 days, oral rehydration salts, and zinc supplementation.

rules.reason: Persistent diarrhoea lasting 14+ days increases malnutrition risk and requires nutritional intervention.
rules.recommended_action: Give multivitamins with zinc for 14 days and counsel mother on appropriate feeding.

rules.reason: Moderate anaemia indicated by some palmar pallor requires iron supplementation and parasite treatment.
rules.recommended_action: Start iron supplementation and mebendazole if child is 1+ years old, return in 14 days.

rules.reason: Acute ear infection requires antibiotic treatment to prevent chronic ear disease.
rules.recommended_action: Give 5-day antibiotic course and paracetamol for pain, return in 5 days.

rules.reason: Chronic ear infection (14+ days drainage) requires quinolone eardrops and ear drying.
rules.recommended_action: Keep ear dry with cotton wicking and use quinolone eardrops for 14 days.

rules.reason: Moderate acute malnutrition requires nutritional counseling and micronutrient supplementation.
rules.recommended_action: Counsel mother on nutrition and appropriate feeding, return in 30 days for reassessment.

rules.reason: Mild cough without fast breathing or chest indrawing indicates simple upper respiratory infection.
rules.recommended_action: Soothe throat with safe home remedy and return in 5 days if not improving.

rules.reason: Low fever without identified cause or danger signs is usually self-limiting.
rules.recommended_action: Give paracetamol if temperature above 38.5°C and return in 2 days if fever persists.

rules.reason: Diarrhoea without dehydration signs is manageable with home care and oral rehydration.
rules.recommended_action: Teach home fluid, zinc and food treatment (Plan A), return in 5 days if not improving.

rules.reason: No danger signs identified during routine assessment.
rules.recommended_action: Provide home care guidance and routine follow-up as indicated.

rules.reason: Airway obstruction or severe respiratory distress is immediately life-threatening.
rules.recommended_action: Clear airway, give oxygen 5L/min immediately, prepare airway management equipment, refer emergently.

rules.reason: Signs of shock or circulatory failure are immediately life-threatening.
rules.recommended_action: Establish two IV lines, begin rapid fluid bolus, give oxygen, control bleeding, refer emergently.

rules.reason: Altered consciousness or current convulsions indicate critical neurological emergency.
rules.recommended_action: Protect from injury, manage airway, check glucose immediately, give diazepam if convulsing, refer emergently.

rules.reason: Significant breathing difficulty requires urgent oxygen and assessment.
rules.recommended_action: Measure oxygen saturation immediately, give oxygen if SpO2 < 90%.

rules.reason: Extreme pallor suggests severe anaemia or circulatory compromise requiring urgent assessment.
rules.recommended_action: Establish IV access, check vital signs and pulse, assess for bleeding or shock.

rules.reason: General appearance of severe acute illness requires urgent evaluation to identify cause.
rules.recommended_action: Take vital signs, perform brief focused assessment, establish IV access if needed.

rules.reason: Recent loss of consciousness requires urgent cardiac and metabolic assessment.
rules.recommended_action: Check blood glucose, take ECG if available, get vital signs, establish IV access.

rules.reason: Active significant bleeding increases shock risk and requires urgent hemorrhage control.
rules.recommended_action: Establish two IV lines with large-bore needles, start rapid IV fluids, identify bleeding source.

rules.reason: Frequent diarrhoea (>5 times daily) raises dehydration risk and possible cholera.
rules.recommended_action: Assess dehydration status, give oral rehydration initially, IV fluids if severe dehydration.

rules.reason: New onset neurological symptoms may indicate acute stroke requiring time-sensitive intervention.
rules.recommended_action: Check blood glucose, assess stroke risk, arrange CT or imaging, consider thrombolytic therapy.

rules.reason: No emergency or priority signs present; patient can wait for routine consultation.
rules.recommended_action: Provide routine care and standard consultation without urgency.
